revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:зЁҡе…җиҲһ]]



'''Chigo-mai''' (зЁҡе…җиҲһ, Chigo no Mai) refers to traditional folk performing arts known as {{ill|Chigo-mai|ja|зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo-mai|qq=}}, involving dances performed by children. The term encompasses a variety of regional dances across Japan, many of which are designated as important intangible folk cultural properties.
* {{ill|Amatsu Shrine|en|Amatsu Shrine|qid=Q172253|lt=Amanokagura Shrine|1=Amatsu Shrine}} in {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|qid=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa City|1=Itoigawa}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. This dance is recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property and is associated with {{ill|Itoigawa-No's Bugaku|ja|зіёйӯҡе·қгғ»иғҪз”ҹгҒ®иҲһжҘҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoigawa-No's Bugaku|1=Itoigawa-No's Bugaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604877}}.
* {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|qid=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} in {{ill|Yahiko, Niigata|en|Yahiko, Niigata|qid=Q1349667|lt=Yahiko Village|1=Yahiko, Niigata}}, {{ill|Nishikanbara District, Niigata|en|Nishikanbara District, Niigata|qid=Q1155139|lt=Nishikanbara District|1=Nishikanbara District, Niigata}}, Niigata Prefecture. This shrine preserves a sacred ritual and festival known as "Yahiko Shrine Lantern Pushing and Bugaku," also designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
* {{ill|EchЕ« no Chigo-mai|ja|и¶ҠдёӯгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/EchЕ« no Chigo-mai|1=Chigo Dance of EtchЕ«|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11636372}} in {{ill|Toyama (city)|en|Toyama (city)|qid=Q204266|lt=Toyama City|1=Toyama (city)}}, {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|en|Kurobe, Toyama|qid=Q753864|lt=Kurobe City|1=Kurobe, Toyama}}, and {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|qid=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. These dances are Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties:
** {{ill|Kamo Shrine Chigo-mai|ja|еҠ иҢӮзҘһзӨҫгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine Chigo-mai|1=Chigo Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398889}}
** {{ill|Asu no Chigo-mai|ja|жҳҺж—ҘгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asu no Chigo-mai|1=Ashita no Chigo Mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512132}}
** {{ill|Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|ja|зҶҠйҮҺзҘһзӨҫгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ (еҜҢеұұеёӮ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|1=Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568967}}
* {{ill|Kawaguchi no Chigo-mai|ja|жІіеҸЈгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaguchi no Chigo-mai|1=Kawaguchi no Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11553660}} in {{ill|River mouth|en|River mouth|qid=Q1233637|lt=Kawaguchi, Fujikawaguchiko|1=River mouth}}, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|en|Fujikawaguchiko|qid=Q1004231|lt=Fujikawaguchiko Town|1=Fujikawaguchiko}}, {{ill|Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi|en|Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi|qid=Q1141178|lt=Minamitsuru District|1=Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. This dance is also an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property.
* {{ill|Hatsukaesai Chigo-mai|ja|е»ҝж—ҘдјҡзҘӯгҒ®зЁҡе…җиҲһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsukaesai Chigo-mai|1=Chigomai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486882}} at {{ill|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|en|Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|qid=Q500368|lt=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine}} in {{ill|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|en|Aoi-ku, Shizuoka|qid=Q1131483|lt=Aoi Ward, Shizuoka|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka (city)|en|Shizuoka (city)|qid=Q174691|lt=Shizuoka City|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. This dance is a Shizuoka Prefecture-designated intangible folk cultural property.
For additional examples of Chigo-mai and related traditions, refer to {{ill|Chigo|ja|зЁҡе…җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo|qq=}}.

{{disambiguation}}

{{translated page|ja|зЁҡе…җиҲһ|version=96726847|comment=Imported full ja history}}
